# copy-public-functions-into-header

This is a simple program to copy function declarations from the .c files into .h files. It reads a .c file and a .h file and prints out the completed header file to stdout. It does this through a simple find and replace. 

For example, running `copy-public-functions-into-header ./test/source.c ./test/source.h` results in the following header file: 
```c
struct a_struct_definition {
    int length;
};

// HEADER DECLARATIONS AUTOGENERATED FROM ./test/source.c
int this_is_a_function(void);
// END OF HEADER DECLARATIONS AUTOGENERATED FROM ./test/source.c
```

source.c
```c
#define PUBLIC

PUBLIC int this_is_a_function(void) {

}
```

source.h 
```c 
struct a_struct_definition {
    int length;
};

// HEADER DECLARATIONS AUTOGENERATED FROM ./test/source.c
// END OF HEADER DECLARATIONS AUTOGENERATED FROM ./test/source.c
```

## Usage 
```bash 
> copy-public-functions-into-header
USAGE:
    ./copy-public-functions-into-header <source> <header> [-h] [-d] 

ARGUMENTS:
    <source>    Source .c file path
    <header>    Header .h file path

OPTIONS:
    -h          Show help
    -d          Print debug messages into stderr
```
